title: centaurea aspera var. scorpiurifolia
intro: Centaurea aspera var. scorpiurifolia, also known as Scorpion's Spur or Rough Centaury, is a plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae) and is characterized by its distinctive, spiny foliage and purplish-blue flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may help to soothe wounds, reduce fever, and combat infections. The health benefits of Centaurea aspera var. scorpiurifolia are thought to include its potential to aid in the healing of skin conditions, such as acne, eczema, and ulcers, as well as its possible role in reducing stress and promoting relaxation. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, or salves, which can be applied topically to the affected area or taken internally under the guidance of a qualified healthcare practitioner.
